UT Systems and Storage Lab
  • Home
  • Publications
  • Awards
  • Team

Welcome!

We are the UT Systems and Storage Lab. We build the next generation of data systems with a focus on performance and reliability. We open-source our software and collaborate closely with industry.


Research group in front of UTCS building in 2025.
L to R: Minh, Talia, Dakshin, Shurang, Nitin, Vijay, Angela, and Dorcas.


Recent Publications
  1. Faster-than-light coordination for networked systems with quantum non-local games
    Venkat Arun, Vijay Chidambaram, Scott Aaronson
    24th ACM Workshop on Hot Topics in Networks (HotNets 2025) 
    @inproceedings{arun2025quantum,
  title = {Faster-than-light coordination for networked systems
                    with quantum non-local games},
  author = {Arun, Venkat and Chidambaram, Vijay and Aaronson, Scott},
  booktitle = {"24th ACM Workshop on Hot Topics in Networks
                    (HotNets 2025)"},
  year = {2025},
  month = nov,
  address = {College Park, Maryland, USA},
  group = {workshop},
  keywords = {quantum},
  pdf = {https://www.cs.utexas.edu/~vijay/papers/hotnets25-quantum.pdf},
  slides = {https://www.cs.utexas.edu/~vijay/slides/hotnets25-quantum-slides.pdf}
}

    

    

    



    2. 

  2. 



    
  
    GPEmu: A GPU Emulator for Faster and Cheaper
                  Prototyping and Evaluation of Deep Learning System
                  Research
  
    
    
    
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
        Meng Wang,

        Naufal Ananda, 
      
    
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
        Yuyang Huang, 
      
    
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
        Kemas Wiharja,

    Proceedings of the VLDB Endowment, Volume 18, Issue 6
  
  
  
    

  
  

  

















  




    

    @inproceedings{wang2025gpemu,
  title = {{GPEmu: A GPU Emulator for Faster and Cheaper
                    Prototyping and Evaluation of Deep Learning System
                    Research}},
  author = {Wang, Meng and Waldspurger, Gus and Ananda, Naufal and Huang, Yuyang and Wiharja, Kemas and Bent, John and Sundararaman, Swaminathan and Chidambaram, Vijay and Gunawi, Haryadi S},
  journal = {Proceedings of the {VLDB} Endowment},
  volume = {18},
  number = {6},
  pages = {1919--1932},
  year = {2025},
  month = sep,
  pdf = {https://vldb.org/pvldb/vol18/p1919-wang.pdf},
  keywords = {ML},
  software = {https://github.com/mengwanguc/gpemu},
  slides = {https://www.cs.utexas.edu/~vijay/slides/vldb25-gpemu-slides.pdf}
}

    

    



    

    

    

    



    3.

        Yan Sun,

        Dixin Tang,

    19th USENIX Symposium on Operating Systems Design
                  and Implementation (OSDI 25)
  
  
  
    

  
  

  

















  




    

    @inproceedings{huang25tigon,
  author = {Huang, Yibo and Chen, Haowei and Ni, Newton and Sun, Yan and Chidambaram, Vijay and Tang, Dixin and Witchel, Emmett},
  title = {{Tigon: A Distributed Database for a CXL Pod}},
  booktitle = {19th USENIX Symposium on Operating Systems Design
                    and Implementation (OSDI 25)},
  year = {2025},
  pdf = {https://www.usenix.org/system/files/osdi25-huang-yibo.pdf},
  publisher = {USENIX Association},
  month = jul,
  keywords = {CXL, databases},
  slides = {https://www.cs.utexas.edu/~vijay/slides/osdi25-huang-yibo-slides.pdf},
  software = {https://github.com/ut-datasys/tigon}
}

    

    



    

    

    

    








  

  
  

	

  
News


  
  Sep 2025
    
Our work on using quantum systems for coordination in distributed systems was accepted to HotNets 25!


  
  Aug 2025
    
Four new students (Minh, Talia, Nitin, and Shurang) are joining the group. Welcome!


  
  Apr 2025
    
Hayley defended her thesis and graduated! She will be joining Microsoft Research. Congratulations!


  
  Mar 2025
    
Tigon and PoWER were accepted to OSDI 25!


  
  Jan 2025
    
GPEmu got accepted to VLDB 25!


  

  
... see all News
